methodology: Initiative-centered crosswalk derived from cited records already in the project. Participation is not treated as equal responsibility, shared funding, or proof of causation. This layer does not change any department goal status.

attributionDefinitions.documented: A source explicitly names the participants and relationship.
attributionDefinitions.mixed: Some participant relationships are explicit and others are cross-referenced or incompletely named.
attributionDefinitions.inferred: Separate records show a shared dependency, but no source describes a formal collaboration.
attributionDefinitions.unresolved: Sources conflict or do not identify enough participants to attribute the work safely.

initiatives.title: EECBG implementation portfolio
initiatives.summary: One $314,150 DOE award supports fleet planning, building studies, greenhouse-gas accounting, conservation outreach, and community incentives across multiple SEP goals.

participants.name: Equipment Services
participants.role: Fleet inventory, purchasing, leasing, and transition analysis

participants.name: People & Culture
participants.role: Uses fleet findings for idling-reduction training

milestones.text: City reported receipt of the award in December.
Funded studies and outreach activities began; USAspending later showed partial outlays.

initiatives.attributionGap: The grant record does not allocate dollars by activity or department, so the dashboard must not assign shares of the award.

initiatives.title: Citywide fleet modernization and telematics
initiatives.summary: Fleet studies, a 100-vehicle telematics pilot, existing vendor systems, purchasing analysis, and driver training form a citywide implementation chain.

participants.name: Seven unnamed departments
participants.role: Telematics pilot participants; identities not fully published

milestones.text: OSR, Equipment Services, and Transportation participated in fleet transition work.
A 100-vehicle pilot across seven departments was compared with roughly 300 vehicles using three other vendors.

initiatives.attributionGap: One local note says Field Operations was included while another correctly notes that the source does not name all seven departments. Field Operations participation remains unresolved pending a primary record.

initiatives.title: Municipal building energy management
initiatives.summary: Energy audits and account-level monitoring support facilities across the City, even though reporting groups the outcome under buildings managed by Engineering & Inspections.

milestones.text: Audits focused on ten high-energy-use buildings while the account platform covered approximately 1,100 electric accounts.

initiatives.attributionGap: The functional building category is not equivalent to one department. Available records do not allocate savings or responsibility across facilities.

initiatives.title: Tree canopy planning and community delivery
initiatives.summary: Grant administration, canopy planning, community engagement, and planting involve City departments, advisory bodies, nonprofits, and residents.

milestones.text: The assessment's comparison baseline was captured from NAIP imagery.
Updated imagery established the ten-year canopy-change endpoint.
USDA urban forestry funding was announced.
OSR held two community meetings, reported three planting events, relaunched the taskforce, and published assessment resources.

evidence.label: 2025 Annual Progress Report, tree canopy section
Greensboro Canopy assessment resources and public ArcGIS tables

citation.title: Keeping the Green in Greensboro — Resources

initiatives.attributionGap: The assessment quantifies citywide and tract canopy conditions and priority areas. It does not assign those observed canopy outcomes to individual departments or partners, so this site shows the documented working relationship without claiming causal credit.

initiatives.title: Household energy and water conservation delivery
initiatives.summary: Weatherization, utility referrals, WaterWise kits, communications, and planned neighborhood programs connect departments and outside delivery partners.

participants.name: Communications & Marketing
participants.role: Education materials identified in the SEP

milestones.text: Housing and Duke Energy programs collaborated on referrals and repairs, including a 59-unit heat-pump project.
WaterWise portal and conservation outreach were reported.

initiatives.attributionGap: Some organizations are named in planned SEP strategies rather than documented completed delivery. The view distinguishes plans from reported milestones.

initiatives.title: White Street renewable-energy asset and Water Resources goal
initiatives.summary: A 4.8 MW solar facility operates on City land under Field Operations while Water Resources' separate 1 MW T.Z. Osborne proposal remained unbuilt, revealing a citywide outcome that department-goal accounting cannot credit cleanly.

participants.name: Field Operations / Solid Waste & Recycling
participants.role: Department associated with the White Street landfill asset

participants.name: Water Resources Department
participants.role: Owner of the separate WR-G3 renewable-energy commitment

milestones.text: Council records document White Street amendments and authorization of a separate T.Z. Osborne lease.
The White Street 4.8 MW array became operational.
SEP reporting still had no mechanism to credit the asset to WR-G3.

initiatives.attributionGap: The cross-reference demonstrates an accounting gap, not that the White Street project fulfilled WR-G3 or that Water Resources controlled the project. The T.Z. Osborne authorization does not prove construction.

initiatives.title: Transportation, Transit, and utility-owned infrastructure
initiatives.summary: GTA's 2023 separation from Transportation and combined 2025 reporting limit departmental attribution, while streetlight progress depends on Duke Energy-owned infrastructure.

participants.name: Transportation Department
participants.role: Streetlight coordination and pre-2023 transit reporting boundary

participants.name: Greensboro Transit Agency
participants.role: Transit fleet implementation as a separate department after 2023

milestones.text: FTA awarded major funding for zero-emission transit.
Electricity progress was reported for Transportation and Transit combined.

initiatives.attributionGap: Combined reporting cannot isolate efficiency changes by the post-reorganization departments, and utility ownership means City coordination is not the same as direct implementation.
